definition: The basic principles or elements of a subject or skill; the essential parts.
example: I've seen you guys can shoot but there's more to the game than shooting. There's fundamentals and defense.

definition: Having or showing the capacity to develop into something in the future; latent qualities or abilities.
example: If you put your effort and concentration into playing to your potential, to be the best that you can be...

definition: The combined action of a group, especially when effective and efficient; functioning as one unit.
example: Teamwork is five players on the floor as one unit—team, team, team—no one more important than the other.

definition: A competitor thought to have little chance of winning; one who is not favored.
example: The underdog forgets the crowds, the school size, the fancy uniforms, and remembers what got them here.

definition: Training that corrects, molds, or perfects the mental faculties or moral character; controlled behavior.
example: Discipline designs practices not for your enjoyment, but for your readiness when the lights come on.

definition: The center of interest or activity; close attention; concentration on the present task.
example: Focus on the fundamentals that we've gone over time and time again.

definition: The transmission of customs or beliefs from generation to generation; a long-established custom or belief.
example: There's a tradition in tournament play to not talk about the next step until you've climbed the one in front of you.

definition: A person or thing that wins something; in Coach Dale's definition, anyone who gives full effort to their potential.
example: I don't care what the scoreboard says at the end of the game, in my book we're gonna be winners.

definition: A group organized to work together.
example: Five players on the floor functioning as one single unit: team, team, team.

definition: To assess size or extent; the basket’s height as a metaphor for constancy.
example: The basket is still ten feet—measure your fear against what has not changed.

definition: Characteristic of a small community; often underestimated.
example: A small-town team can still walk into a palace if their habits are bigger than the building.

definition: To throw the ball to a teammate; to prioritize the group.
example: The best shot is sometimes the pass you almost refused to make.

definition: Continuous physical or mental force; stress of competition.
example: Pressure is a privilege when you trained for the moment it arrives.

definition: Trust or confidence in a person, team, or idea.
example: Belief starts on the practice floor, long before the gym ever fills.

definition: A firmly held belief or opinion.
example: Conviction without evidence is noise; conviction after struggle is leadership.

definition: The quality of being coherent and intelligible.
example: Clarity is kindness in speech—confusion is often a way to hide.
